[00:00–00:30]
I call this regular meeting of the Spring Branch Independent School District Board of Trustees to
order. Let the record show that the time is now six o'clock and a quorum of board members is
present. This meeting has been duly called, and the notice for this meeting has been posted in
accordance with the Texas Open Meetings Act, Texas Government Code Chapter 551041. Please stand
while Trustee David Slattery leads us in the Pledge of Allegiance to the U.S. and Texas flags.

[00:30–01:00]
I would like to introduce the senior administrative staff. They are Miss Linda Buckman.

[01:00–01:30]
Buckman, Associate Superintendent for Communications and Community Engagement, Ms. Karen Heath,
Associate Superintendent for Administration and Talent, Ms. Christina Masick, Associate
Superintendent for Information Technology, Dr. Annie Wolf, Associate Superintendent for Academics,
Ms. Christine Porter, Associate Superintendent for Finance, Mr. Travis Stanford, Associate
Superintendent for Operations, Dr. Jennifer Parker, Community Superintendent, and Dr. Brian
Williams, Community Superintendent. I would like to introduce your board members from left to right.

[01:30–02:00]
They are board member Courtney Anderson, Board Member David Slattery, Stacey Kim, Executive
Assistant to the Board, Dr. Jennifer Blaine, Superintendent. I'm Shannon Mayhan, Board President,
Board Vice President Caroline H. Bennett, Board Secretary Walker Akneo Jr., Board Member Chris
Ernest, and Board Member Jennifer Highland. The first item on the agenda is prayer. Please stand and
join Trustee Chris Ernest in prayer. Lord, we just ask for your presence. We ask for

[02:00–02:30]
wisdom and discernment as we govern over the school district. Lord, we're grateful for all of the
students that you've entrusted to our care, Lord God. We just pray you give us the resources
necessary to provide a quality education for them. In Jesus' name I pray. Amen. Dr. Blaine, do you
have any opening remarks? I do actually, thank you. So we want to welcome our students, employees,
families, friends, and community to the board meeting tonight. We're so glad you all are able to
come out tonight and help us celebrate and congratulate our amazing students and employees.

[02:30–03:00]
Last Thursday, as an update, we had an opportunity to celebrate 20 fantastic years of our Good
Neighbor program. Hundreds of students, staff, and community members came out for a fun night of
food, fun celebration, and got to see an amazing football game between Northbrook High School and
Memorial High School. So I just want to express appreciation for this event to Linda Buckman,
Associate Superintendent for Communications and Community Engagement, and her entire communications
team. This is a really large event, and it was extremely well attended. And I've heard lots, heard
lots of feedback from community members about how much they loved it.

[03:00–03:30]
And I think it gave a lot of us an opportunity to get to know some of our younger families that we
don't always get to interact with. And so it was a lot of fun. And thank you for those of you that
came out. And under the special presentations portion of our agenda, you are going to see some
highlights from that fun event. And then the last thing is when we speak about our two highest
priorities in Spring Branch, they are the safety of our students and staff and the academic growth
and achievement of our students. And tonight we're going to have the opportunity to underscore both
of these priorities with the recognition of the following our campus safety offers, which I
officers, which I see many of them.

and Pam Metcalf, who works in Karen's division, as an executive director for administration safety

basis and communicate with our campus safety officers. For those of you that don't know, campus
safety officers are like a complement to the police department. So we have our police department
with led by Chief Bainbridge and our officers with the legal side of that, but we also have the
campus.

to do. Every one of these campus safety officers are here tonight. They show professionalism, they

The next item is public comment on agenda or non agenda items. At board meetings, public comment
time is set aside for individuals who wish to address the board. During special meetings, workshops,
and retreats, comments are limited to the agenda items only. At regular meetings, individuals may
comment on both agenda and non-agenda items. Individuals who wish to participate during the portion
of the meeting designated for public comment shall sign up by completing the appropriate form on the
district website, by submitting the form in person at the administration building, or by sending an
email with the district form as an attack.

[24:00–24:30]
District form as an attachment to the email address listed on the form. Public comment forms must be
received no later than 12 p.m. noon on the day of the meeting and shall indicate the agenda item or
topic on which they would like to address the board. The length of public comment shall be at the
discretion of the board president not to exceed four minutes. We will do four minutes today. Because
individual rights are protected by various laws and board policies, complaints regarding specific
employees or students should be directed to the administration. If a person makes complaints about a
specific employee or student during public comment, the board

[24:30–25:00]
president has the discretion to move the board into closed session to hear such complaints. If
anyone makes a complaint about a specific employee by name or by job title, the board may hold the
public comment until the end of public comments and hear all such complaints individually in closed
session. Under the law, the board cannot deliberate, discuss, or make a decision on any subject that
is not on the agenda. So as a general matter, the board does not respond to questions at public
comment. Specific factual information or review of existing policy, however, may where legally
allowed be provided in response.
